title: Block Insert and Append
subtitle: I and A in block mode = insert/append on every selected line.
summary: After Ctrl-V selecting a column-rectangle, I types text inserted at the start of every line in the block; A appends it. The text is inserted on all lines after Esc.

blocks.text: This is the trick most people don't know exists — and once you know it, you'll use it constantly.

sequence.note: Insert at the LEFT edge of the block
Now the prefix appears on every selected line

blocks.title: The Magic Trick
blocks.text:
While you're typing in Insert mode, the prefix **only appears on the top line**. It's after you hit {key:Esc} that Vim *replays* the insertion on every other line in the original block. The replay only happens for inserts started with capital {key:I} or {key:A} from block Visual — lowercase {key:i}/{key:a} from block mode just enter normal Insert.

**{key:I}** inserts at the **left edge** of the block. **{key:A}** inserts past the **right edge** of the block.

**{key:c}** (change) replaces the block's contents on every line — start typing once, on Esc all rows get the new text.

**{key:r}{c}** fills the block with character `c`.

blocks.title: Ragged Right Edges
blocks.text: When you select a block past the end of a short line, those rows have a *virtual* right edge. {key:A} can still append there — Vim pads with spaces as needed, but only on rows where you actually inserted text. If `'virtualedit'` is set to `block`, you can extend the cursor anywhere on screen, even past line ends, and the math just works.
Need to comment out a block of code? {key:Ctrl-V}{count}{key:j}{key:I}{key://}{key: }{key:Esc} — five keystrokes.
